title: Common Coding Agent Pitfalls
concept: No sandboxing, poor compiler feedback parsing, infinite debugging loops, and ignoring security alerts.

exercises.prompt: What is one important item to verify when creating a secure coding agent?

options.text: It can connect to any network.

exercises.explain: Sandboxing helps keep the coding agent secure by restricting its environment.

cards.title: Common Pitfalls in Coding Agents
cards.body: Coding agents can make mistakes that lead to problems in their performance and security. Let's learn about some common pitfalls.

cards.title: No Sandboxing
cards.body: Not using a sandbox means your coding agent can access too much. This can lead to accidental damage or security risks.

cards.title: Sandboxing is Like a Playground
cards.body: Think of sandboxing like a playground for coding agents. Just as kids can play safely in a fenced area, coding agents need boundaries to stay secure.

cards.title: Poor Compiler Feedback
cards.body: When a coding agent doesn't understand error messages or warnings, it might miss important fixes. This is like ignoring someone's advice when you need help.

cards.title: Debunking Debugging Loops
cards.body: It's a myth that all bugs can be fixed by endlessly looking for them. Sometimes you need to take a break and rethink your approach.

cards.title: Agent Pitfalls Illustrated
cards.body: Illustrating common pitfalls helps highlight their impact. Let's look at sandboxes and security alerts!

exercises.prompt: What happens if a coding agent ignores security alerts?

options.text: It can become vulnerable to attacks.
It always finds the bugs first.

exercises.explain: Ignoring security alerts puts the coding agent in danger, making it open to attacks and vulnerabilities.

exercises.prompt: Ignoring compiler feedback can make coding easier.
exercises.explain: Ignoring feedback from the compiler actually makes coding harder, as it leads to more mistakes and confusion.

exercises.prompt: It's dangerous if a coding agent doesn't use __________.
exercises.sentence: It's dangerous if a coding agent doesn't use sandboxing.
exercises.explain: Sandboxing is crucial for keeping coding agents safe from external threats.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is a common coding agent pitfall?
exercises.explain: Not reviewing error messages is a pitfall that can lead to unresolved problems in your coding agent.

exercises.prompt: What should a coding agent do to avoid overlooking critical issues?

options.text: Ignore alerts to speed things up.
Use the same code without changes.

exercises.explain: Carefully reading all compiler feedback helps the coding agent learn and improve, preventing critical issues from being missed.
